mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, it signals a severe labor market deterioration that forces corporate earnings downgrades and triggers risk-off portfolio rebalancing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return difference is statistically indistinguishable from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint expansion signal fails to produce a positive 60-day return in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces near-term corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week when inflation remains elevated.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (accuracy < 50%) over a 2-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal yields a negative 5-day return in more than 55% of observations, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and 5-day returns falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 550 thousand units, it confirms a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which reduces downstream economic activity and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the conditional mean return is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05) in a bootstrap test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the consumer price index and the federal funds rate confirm an active monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint tightening signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index during periods of low unemployment indicates robust capacity utilization and strong order backlogs, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the signal information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
